content.full_text:
June 13, 1977

Nicholas D. Bevilacqua  
District Assistant  
367 Civic Drive  
Pleasant Hill, California 94523

Dear Mr. Bevilacqua:

You ask whether there is "...any conflict of interest or any other problem with State political or administrative rules if a person works part-time for a Federal Congressman, and part-time with a State of California Legislator, in a staff position."

Based upon the foregoing, no conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act and its conflict of interest prohibition is apparent.

Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103 provide that a California public official may not make or participate in the making of a governmental decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect upon a source of income to the public official. While the Federal government is a source of income to the part-time employee of the Federal Congressman, it is not reasonably foreseeable that the part-time California Legislative aide could make or participate in the making of a decision which could have a material financial effect upon the Federal government.

The part-time Legislative aide in question should consult with the Office of the California Legislative Counsel for the purpose of determining whether a potential conflict of interest exists in the situation presented by virtue of the aide's inclusion in the conflict of interest code of the California Legislature. In addition, the aide should be advised that the foregoing advice includes only advice pursuant to the Political Reform Act and not any other ethical or conflict of interest provision of California Law.

This letter does not constitute staff advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b).

If I may be of further assistance to you in this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916)322-6444.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

DLS:mfa

8 June 1977

Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Committee

STATE AND FEDERAL GOVERNMENT EMPLOYMENT

Is there any conflict of interest or any other problem with State political or administrative rules if a person works part-time for a Federal Congressman, and part-time with a State of California Legislator, in a staff position.

Would you send me a written decision please,

Nicholas D. Bevilacqua
District Assistant
367 Civic Drive
Pleasant Hill, CA 94523
